---
title: "QWA"
author: "Louis Verschuren"
date: "2026-06-15"
output: 
     html_document:
          theme: yeti
          code_folding: hide
          toc: true
          toc_depth: 3
          toc_float:
            collapsed: false
          number_sections: true
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  out.width = '100%',
  dpi=300
)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
remove(list = ls())

#### load necessary (and usefull) packages ####

library("tidyverse")
library("dplyr")
library("ggplot2")
library("readxl")
library("writexl")
library("data.table")




# XCT.read setup
source("XCT.Read.R") # load XCT.read function (download here: https://github.com/UGent-Woodlab/XCT.read-R-function)



```




# Settings
```{r}
overruleResolution = TRUE
resolution = 0.2 # pixel resolution in microns
area <- c(0.75, 1) # area of the ring to calculate fractions over (0-1)
```





# load QWA data
```{r}
# Load QWA data profiles and calculate mean densities
Vesseldata <- XCT.read("segmentations/Vessels", output = "ringwidth_density", densityType = "fraction", area = area, fun = "mean", overruleResolution = overruleResolution, resolution = resolution) %>% rename(Vessels = Density) %>% select(Sample, Year, Vessels, RW)
Raydata <- XCT.read("segmentations/Rays", output = "ringwidth_density", densityType = "fraction", area = area, fun = "mean", overruleResolution = overruleResolution, resolution = resolution) %>% rename(Rays = Density) %>% select(Sample, Year, Rays)
Fiberdata <- XCT.read("segmentations/Fibers", output = "ringwidth_density", densityType = "fraction", area = area, fun = "mean", overruleResolution = overruleResolution, resolution = resolution) %>% rename(FiberLumen = Density) %>% select(Sample, Year, FiberLumen)
FiberWalldata <- XCT.read("segmentations/FiberWalls", output = "ringwidth_density", densityType = "fraction", area = area, fun = "mean", overruleResolution = overruleResolution, resolution = resolution) %>% rename(FiberWalls = Density) %>% select(Sample, Year, FiberWalls)

# Load vessel conductivity data
Conductivitydata <- read.csv("segmentations/Hydraulic conductivity/all_vessel_properties.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",", dec = ".") %>% rename(Sample = image)

# path to ring indication data vessel
path <- "segmentations/Vessels"
```



# load hydraulic conductivity data and ring data, assign rings to vessels
```{r}

# Load all ringwidth.txt files
files <- list.files(path, pattern = "_ringwidth.txt", full.names = TRUE)

# resolution in mm
resolution_mm = resolution*1000 

# Function to read each file, add the file name, and combine all into one data frame
rings <- lapply(files, function(file) {
  # Extract the file name before "_density_corr"
  file_name <- sub("_ringwidth.txt$", "", basename(file))
  
  # Read the file, treat "NaN" as NA, and specify numeric columns
  data <- read_delim(file, delim = ", ", 
                     col_names = c("width", "Year", "pixelsize", "Felldate", "MissingringsBefore", "BrokenRingType"),
                     na = "NaN", show_col_types = FALSE)  
  data <- data %>% mutate(Sample = file_name, row_number = row_number()) # Add the file name as a new column
  data <- data %>% filter(BrokenRingType != 2) # remove type 2 broken rings rows
  data <- data %>% group_by(Year) %>% mutate(width = max(width)) %>% ungroup() # set ringwidth to the max ringwidth: relevant for ring a type 1 broken ring present
  data <- data %>% filter(BrokenRingType != 1) # remove type 1 broken rings rows
  if (overruleResolution) {data$pixelsize <- resolution_mm} # set the resolution to the value in the resolution parameter
  data$RW <- data$width * data$pixelsize /1000 # calculate ring width in mm
  return(data)
}) %>% bind_rows() # Combine all data frames into one


# Load all _zpos files
files <- list.files(path, pattern = "_zpos.txt", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to read each file, add the file name, and combine all into one data frame
zpos_corr <- lapply(files, function(file) {
  # Extract the file name before "_density_corr"
  file_name <- sub("_zpos.txt$", "", basename(file))
  
  # Read the file, treat "NaN" as NA
  data <- read_delim(file, delim = "\n", col_names = "xpos", na = "NaN",  show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  # Add the file name as a new column
  data <- data %>% mutate(Sample = file_name, row_number = row_number()-1)
  
  return(data)
}) %>% bind_rows() # Combine all data frames into one


# Load all _ring_and_fibre.txt files
files <- list.files(path, pattern = "_ring_and_fibre.txt", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to read each file, add the file name, and combine all into one data frame
ring_and_fibre <- lapply(files, function(file) {
  # Extract the file name before "_density_corr"
  file_name <- sub("_ring_and_fibre.txt$", "", basename(file))
  
  # Read the file, treat "NaN" as NA
  data <- read_delim(file, delim = ", ", col_names = c("xposEndRing", "angle", "angle2"), na = "NaN",  show_col_types = FALSE) 
  data$angle2 <- NULL
  
  # Add the file name as a new column
  data <- data %>% mutate(Sample = file_name, row_number = row_number()-1)
  
  return(data)
}) %>% bind_rows() # Combine all data frames into one


# Merge rings and zpos_corr by Sample and row_number to add start and end columns
rings <- rings %>%
  left_join(zpos_corr %>% 
              mutate(row_number = row_number + 1) %>% # Shift row_number for "start"
              rename(start = xpos), 
            by = c("Sample", "row_number")) %>%
  left_join(zpos_corr %>% rename(end = xpos), by = c("Sample", "row_number")) %>%
  left_join(ring_and_fibre %>% rename(angleEndRing = angle), by = c("Sample", "row_number"))
rings$start <- rings$start + 1 # first pixel is the pixel after the end of the last one, otherwise this pixel is accounted twice
rings <- rings %>% na.omit()   # remove NAs
ringsbackup <- rings

# remove duplicate rings
rings <- rings %>%
  group_by(Sample, Year) %>%
  slice_max((end - start), with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()



# fast method to find the angle of the closest ring
# Convert to data.tables
data_dt <- as.data.table(Conductivitydata)
rings_dt <- as.data.table(rings)
# Create ID for later reordering
data_dt[, id := .I]
# Create midpoint if needed (or use 'end')
rings_dt[, match_pos := end]  # or midpoint: (start + end)/2
# Sort both by Sample and match_pos
setkey(rings_dt, Sample, match_pos)
setkey(data_dt, Sample, centroid_x_px)
# Perform rolling join
result <- rings_dt[data_dt, on = .(Sample, match_pos = centroid_x_px), roll = "nearest"]
# Restore original row order
setorder(result, id)
# Assign back
Conductivitydata$angle <- result$angleEndRing






# now each vessel need to be projected to a new x position in the middle line of the sample 
Conductivitydata$Xproj <- Conductivitydata$centroid_x_px + tan(Conductivitydata$angle)*((Conductivitydata$image_height_px/2)-Conductivitydata$centroid_y_px)








# Plotting the projection of vessels for a specific sample to check the transformation
df_sample <- Conductivitydata %>% filter(Sample == "S+_22_B")

# # small section of that sample
# df_sample <- df_sample %>% filter(centroid_x_px > 2000 & centroid_x_px < 7000) # adjust the range as needed

# Calculate middle y once
middle_y <- unique(df_sample$image_height_px) / 2

# plot
testplot <- ggplot(df_sample, aes()) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = centroid_x_px, y = centroid_y_px, 
                   xend = Xproj, yend = middle_y),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), color = "blue", alpha = 0.3) +
  
  geom_point(aes(x = centroid_x_px, y = centroid_y_px, size = area_px2), 
             color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +  # original points sized by area_m2
  
  geom_point(aes(x = Xproj, y = middle_y, size = area_px2), 
             color = "green", alpha = 0.3) + # projected points sized by area_m2
  
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 6)) +  # adjust dot size range as needed
  
  labs(title = paste("Projection of vessels - Sample:L-1008-1-B"),
       x = "X position (pixels)",
       y = "Y position (pixels)",
       size = "Area (pxÂ˛)") +
  theme_minimal()

testplot










# assign each vessel to a ring using start and end in rings and Xproj
# Convert to data.tables
data_dt <- as.data.table(Conductivitydata)
rings_dt <- as.data.table(rings)

# Set keys for join by Sample, and sort rings by start
setkey(rings_dt, Sample, start, end)
setkey(data_dt, Sample, Xproj)

# Perform a non-equi join: find for each vessel the ring where start <= Xproj <= end
assigned <- rings_dt[data_dt, 
                    on = .(Sample, start < Xproj, end >= Xproj),
                    nomatch = 0,  # drop vessels that don't match any ring
                    allow.cartesian = TRUE]

Conductivitydata <- data.frame(assigned) %>% select(Sample, Year, end, area_px2, d4_px4, image_height_px) %>% rename(Xproj = end) # keep only relevant columns
rings <- rings %>% select(Sample, Year, start, end) # keep only relevant columns
Conductivitydata <- full_join(Conductivitydata, rings, by = c("Sample", "Year")) # join the two dataframes





```




# merge and export
```{r}
# merge QWA data
Data <- full_join(Raydata, Vesseldata, by = c("Sample", "Year"))
Data <- full_join(Data, Fiberdata, by = c("Sample", "Year"))
Data <- full_join(Data, FiberWalldata, by = c("Sample", "Year"))


# convert to fraction
Data$total <- Data$Vessels + Data$Rays + Data$FiberLumen + Data$FiberWalls
Data$Vessels <- Data$Vessels / Data$total
Data$Rays <- Data$Rays / Data$total
Data$FiberLumen <- Data$FiberLumen / Data$total
Data$FiberWalls <- Data$FiberWalls / Data$total
Data$total <- NULL



# calculate corrected area fractions (excluding rays)
Data$VesselsCorrectedNoRays <- Data$Vessels/(1-Data$Rays)
Data$FiberWallsCorrectedNoRays <- Data$FiberWalls/(1-Data$Rays)
Data$FiberLumenCorrectedNoRays <- Data$FiberLumen/(1-Data$Rays)


# calculate anatomical fiber density
Data$FiberDensity <- Data$FiberWalls/(Data$FiberWalls + Data$FiberLumen) # (is actualy the same as fiber wall fraction)

# calculate anatomical density overall
Data$AnatomicalDensity <- Data$FiberWalls/(Data$FiberWalls + Data$FiberLumen + Data$Vessels)











# merge conductivity data
Conductivitydata <- full_join(Data, Conductivitydata, by = c("Sample", "Year"))


# calculate ringwidth in m for conductivity calculations (temporary, will be deleted later)
Conductivitydata$RWtemp_m <- (Conductivitydata$end - Conductivitydata$start) * resolution/1000000 # in m

# calculate the area of the needed fraction the ring, substract the area of the rays
Conductivitydata$ringSectionArea_m2 <- Conductivitydata$RWtemp_m * (max(area)-min(area)) * (Conductivitydata$image_height_px * resolution/1000000)  * (1-Conductivitydata$Rays) # in mÂ˛

# calculate the relative location of a vessel within the ring
Conductivitydata$relPos <- (Conductivitydata$Xproj - Conductivitydata$start) / (Conductivitydata$end - Conductivitydata$start) # relative position of the vessel in the ring


# constants
viscosityWater <- 0.001

# calculate hydraulic conductivity of the needed fraction the ring
Conductivitydata <- Conductivitydata %>% 
  filter(relPos > min(area) & relPos <= max(area)) %>% # keep only the needed fraction the ring
  group_by(Sample, Year) %>%
  summarise(
    SumD4_m4 = sum(d4_px4)*((resolution/1000000)^4), # sum of the D4_px4 in m4 values of all vessels for each ring in each sample
    ringSectionArea_m2 = mean(ringSectionArea_m2),  # assuming this is consistent per ring
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Ks = ((pi) / (128 * viscosityWater)) * SumD4_m4 / ringSectionArea_m2 * 1000000 # calculate hydraulic conductivity in mÂ˛/sMPa
  )

# merge Ks back to Data
Data <- full_join(Data, Conductivitydata, by = c("Sample", "Year"))










# save the data
write.csv(Data, "Output.csv", row.names = FALSE)



```
